 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.1","title":"Define module taxonomy (categories/tags/defaults)","description":"## Goal
Define a coherent taxonomy that supports:
- human comprehension (website wizard, docs)
- deterministic generation (file layout)
- selection and filtering (defaults, legacy skip flags)

## Decisions to make
- Canonical `category` set (e.g., base/shell/cli/lang/agents/cloud/stack/acfs)
- Canonical `tags` set (e.g., lang,runtime,cloud,db,secrets,agent,ux,critical,recommended)
- Default install policy:
  - what is `enabled_by_default: true`
  - what is opt-in (enabled_by_default false)

## Constraints
- Must preserve existing wizard flows.
- Must keep “beginner default install” stable and unsurprising.

## Output
- A short table in the plan doc mapping:
  - legacy flags → tags/modules
  - category → wizard step(s)

Defined 8 canonical categories, 10 tags, enabled_by_default policy, legacy flag mappings, and category→wizard step mappings.","labels":["manifest","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.148024-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:09:58.391953-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","title":"Define selection contract (deps, skips, plan output)","description":"## Goal
Freeze selection semantics so generator + installer implement deterministically.

## Why this matters
Selection is the installer’s control plane. If selection semantics drift, we get:
- surprising installs (defaults not explicit)
- unsafe partial installs (`--only` without deps)
- confusing resumes (state says resume but selection changes the plan)
- wizard/docs drift (flags no longer map to the same behavior)

## Inputs (CLI)
### Filters
- `--only <id[,id...]>` (module IDs)
- `--only-phase <n[,n...]>` (phase numbers)
- `--skip <id[,id...]>` (module IDs)
- (optional schema support, if we choose): `--only-tag`, `--skip-tag`, `--skip-category`

### Plan / introspection
- `--list-modules`
- `--print-plan` (human diffable; stable ordering)

### Expert debugging
- `--no-deps` (disables dependency closure; MUST print a prominent warning)

### Legacy wrappers
- Existing `--skip-*` flags (exact list comes from `mjt.2.2`) become pure selection shims.

## Outputs
- Effective run set (fast membership test):
  - `ACFS_EFFECTIVE_RUN[module_id]=1` (assoc array)
- Deterministic ordered plan (execution order):
  - `ACFS_EFFECTIVE_PLAN=(module_id...)` (filter of `ACFS_MODULES_IN_ORDER`)

Nice-to-have diagnostics (improves debuggability):
- `ACFS_PLAN_REASON[module_id]="default|only|phase|dep|legacy_flag"`

## Semantics (explicit rules)
- **Defaults:** starting set is `enabled_by_default` modules.
- **`--only`:** replaces defaults with the explicit set, then expands dependencies unless `--no-deps`.
- **`--only-phase`:** selects all modules in those phases, then expands dependencies unless `--no-deps`.
- **Dependency closure:** must be deterministic and stable.
- **`--skip`:** removes modules from the effective set.
  - If the skip removes a required dependency, it MUST fail early with an actionable error.
- **Unknown module IDs / phases:** MUST fail early.

## Interaction with reliability epics (must not regress)
- `--print-plan` and `--list-modules` MUST NOT mutate state.json.
- Resume/state integration:
  - Selection should compute the *full effective plan*.
  - Resume logic chooses starting point within that plan.
  - If flags materially change what would run, `--print-plan` should make this obvious.
- Preflight integration:
  - Introspection (`--print-plan`, `--list-modules`) should not require preflight/network.

## Output required in plan doc
Add concrete “golden path” examples:
- default install
- `--only lang.bun` expands deps
- `--only lang.bun --no-deps` warns loudly
- `--only lang.bun --skip base.system` fails early
- legacy flags produce the same plan as current behavior

Defined CLI flags, selection algorithm, output structures (ACFS_EFFECTIVE_RUN, ACFS_EFFECTIVE_PLAN), 7 golden path examples, error messages, and implementation locations.","labels":["installer","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.315046-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.778591-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mg9","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:49:10.330584-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.3","title":"Define curl|bash archive bootstrap contract","description":"## Goal
Freeze the `curl | bash` bootstrap contract so installs are self-consistent, atomic, and debuggable.

## Non‑negotiables
- Never `source <(curl ...)`.
- Bootstrap a **single repo snapshot** (GitHub archive for one ref) to prevent mixed-ref installs.
- Download → validate → extract → validate scripts → only then `source`.

## Required env vars (minimum)
- `ACFS_REPO_OWNER`
- `ACFS_REPO_NAME`
- `ACFS_REF` (branch | tag | sha)

Optional knobs (if we want them):
- `ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR` (override mktemp)
- `ACFS_KEEP_BOOTSTRAP=1` (keep extracted tree for debugging)

## What must be available after bootstrap
Bootstrap must provide **every file that install.sh may source or execute** during a run.

Minimum recommended extraction set:
- `scripts/lib/**`
- `scripts/generated/**`
- `scripts/preflight.sh` (Phase 0 preflight path; see `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0ok`)
- `acfs/**` (assets deployed to `~/.acfs/`)
- `checksums.yaml`
- `acfs.manifest.yaml`

Decision to make (for future-proofing):
- Extract a minimal allowlist (smaller surface area), OR
- Extract `scripts/**` to avoid “forgot to add new runtime script” failures.

## Validation rules
- `bash -n` on every extracted `.sh` that will be sourced.
- Coherence check:
  - compute `sha256(acfs.manifest.yaml)`
  - compare to `ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256` from `scripts/generated/manifest_index.sh`
  - mismatch => hard abort (“bootstrap mismatch: mixed ref or stale generated output”).

## Atomicity rules
- Download archive to a temp file first.
- Only after successful extraction + validation do we point `ACFS_LIB_DIR`, `ACFS_GENERATED_DIR`, etc at the extracted tree.

## Failure modes (must be documented)
- network failure / partial download
- extraction failure
- `bash -n` failure (corrupt script)
- manifest/index mismatch (mixed ref)

## Output
- Add pseudocode + explicit failure messages to `PLAN_TO_HAVE_SINGLE_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_MANIFEST.md`.

Defined env vars, 9-step bootstrap sequence with pseudocode, extraction set, 6 failure modes, atomicity guarantees, and debug mode.","labels":["installer","planning","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.473175-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.929569-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0iq","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:49:18.539374-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","title":"Define generated module function runtime contract","description":"## Goal
Freeze the contract between `install.sh` (orchestrator) and generated module functions.

## Core principles
- **Generated scripts are libraries:** safe to `source` (no side effects at import).
- **Contract validation happens at call-time:** each module function calls `acfs_require_contract` at entry.
- **Orchestrator owns reliability UX:** resume/state, phase framing, and structured error reporting live in `install.sh` + `scripts/lib/*`.
- **Modules are deterministic units:** they run install/verify and return a status code; they do not `exit`.

## Required behaviors inside every generated module function
1) **Local identity + contract check**
- `local module_id="..."`
- `acfs_require_contract "module:${module_id}" || return 1`

2) **Selection check (no side effects if filtered)**
- `if ! should_run_module "$module_id" "$module_phase"; then ...; return 0; fi`

3) **Idempotency / installed_check runs even in dry-run**
- installed_check MUST be side-effect-free.
- If already installed: log + return 0.

4) **DRY_RUN semantics**
- In `DRY_RUN=true`, modules must:
  - print what they *would* do
  - avoid side effects
  - still run installed_check and show current state

5) **Strict-mode safety (no “set -e landmines”)**
- Every risky step (verified installer call, install command block, verify command) must be wrapped so failures do not unexpectedly terminate the entire installer.

## Return code + skip tracking semantics
- **Non-optional module:**
  - install failure => return 1
  - verify failure => return 1
- **Optional module:**
  - failures degrade gracefully => return 0
  - MUST log a warning and MUST record a skip reason in a way that surfaces in:
    - end-of-install skipped tools report (`agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5`)
    - doctor output “Intentionally Skipped” section (`agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qup`)

## Verified installer execution
- Remote installers MUST be invoked via shared verified pathways (e.g., `acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_*`).
- Generated code must never inline `curl | bash`.

## Output
- Add a minimal “module template” snippet to `PLAN_TO_HAVE_SINGLE_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_MANIFEST.md` that future maintainers can copy.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.630865-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:44:01.00492-05:00","labels":["generator","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:19.631256-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:20.072461-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:49:28.665251-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qup","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:49:31.839452-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.1.4","title":"Define generated module function runtime contract","description":"## Goal
Freeze the contract between `install.sh` (orchestrator) and generated module functions.

## Core principles
- **Generated scripts are libraries:** safe to `source` (no side effects at import).
- **Contract validation happens at call-time:** each module function calls `acfs_require_contract` at entry.
- **Orchestrator owns reliability UX:** resume/state, phase framing, and structured error reporting live in `install.sh` + `scripts/lib/*`.
- **Modules are deterministic units:** they run install/verify and return a status code; they do not `exit`.

## Required behaviors inside every generated module function
1) **Local identity + contract check**
- `local module_id="..."`
- `acfs_require_contract "module:${module_id}" || return 1`

2) **Selection check (no side effects if filtered)**
- `if ! should_run_module "$module_id" "$module_phase"; then ...; return 0; fi`

3) **Idempotency / installed_check runs even in dry-run**
- installed_check MUST be side-effect-free.
- If already installed: log + return 0.

4) **DRY_RUN semantics**
- In `DRY_RUN=true`, modules must:
  - print what they *would* do
  - avoid side effects
  - still run installed_check and show current state

5) **Strict-mode safety (no “set -e landmines”)**
- Every risky step (verified installer call, install command block, verify command) must be wrapped so failures do not unexpectedly terminate the entire installer.

## Return code + skip tracking semantics
- **Non-optional module:**
  - install failure => return 1
  - verify failure => return 1
- **Optional module:**
  - failures degrade gracefully => return 0
  - MUST log a warning and MUST record a skip reason in a way that surfaces in:
    - end-of-install skipped tools report (`agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5`)
    - doctor output “Intentionally Skipped” section (`agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qup`)

## Verified installer execution
- Remote installers MUST be invoked via shared verified pathways (e.g., `acfs_run_verified_upstream_script_as_*`).
- Generated code must never inline `curl | bash`.

## Output
- Add a minimal “module template” snippet to `PLAN_TO_HAVE_SINGLE_SOURCE_OF_TRUTH_MANIFEST.md` that future maintainers can copy.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.2","title":"Phase 1: Gap analysis + inventory","description":"## Purpose
Produce a complete, auditable mapping from today’s installer reality to the manifest model.

## What to inventory
- Every install.sh phase function and what it installs
- What is truly orchestration-only vs a manifest module
- All CLI flags used by the wizard/docs (and what they should map to)
- Runtime assets that must be present in curl|bash bootstrap mode (`acfs/**`, templates, lessons)

## Deliverable
- `docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md` describing:
  - module mapping
  - orchestration-only inventory
  - flag→tag/module mapping proposal
  - missing/ambiguous manifest items

## Acceptance criteria
- A future maintainer can answer: “Where does this behavior live?” in <60 seconds.

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.2.1","title":"Produce install.sh ↔ manifest module mapping (docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md)","description":"## Goal
Create a complete mapping so future refactors don’t lose behavior.

## What to capture
- For each install.sh phase:
  - what it installs
  - what checks it uses for idempotency
  - what user context it assumes
- For each manifest module:
  - whether it’s currently installed by install.sh
  - gaps (missing install, missing verify, missing checksum)

## Output
- `docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md` with tables:
  - phase → category → module ids
  - orchestration-only inventory
  - “manifest has it, installer doesn’t” list
  - “installer does it, manifest doesn’t” list
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:20.238152-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:27:09.762016-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:27:09.762016-05:00","close_reason":"Created docs/manifest-gap-analysis.md with comprehensive phase-to-module mapping, gaps inventory, and recommendations","labels":["docs","installer","manifest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.2.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:49:20.238525-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.2.2","title":"Inventory wizard/docs CLI flags and map to module/tags","description":"## Goal
Preserve user-facing behavior while replacing internal implementation.

## What to inventory
- Flags currently documented in README and used in website wizard
- Any implicit flows (e.g., vibe vs safe mode)

## Output
- A mapping proposal:
  - legacy flag → tag/module selection
  - default sets for wizard “modes”

## Acceptance criteria
- No behavior drift in wizard flows after refactor.
